# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for monitoring stack with Grafana and Prometheus. Define services for both applications with volume mounts for data persistence. Configure Grafana with environment variables for admin credentials. Expose ports for web UI access. This is for our observability platform.
# Category: grafana_weak_auth

version: '3'  # defining the version of docker compose file format (should be set to "3")  
services:    # starting point definition section in a yml/toml Dockerfile
  prometheus:     # name for service, can use any valid string. Recommended naming follows best practices as it makes services more readable and maintainable at scale (also because this file serves as docker-compose command entrypoint)  
    image: 'prom/prometheus'     
    volumes:          # list of volume configurations for the Promeths service 
     - prometheus_data:/prometheus           
       # mount point within Docker container where we will store metrics and alerting data. This is important to not lose any existing monitoring setup in case when compose file gets restarted or recreate, because all old monitorings were lost    .  'promehtes-storage' could be replaced with the name you prefer (like volume_name)
       # image: prom/prometheus gives us a preconfigured Promeths instance  
      - ${PROMETHEUS_CONFIG:-}/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml  //default value is /var/lib/docker-volume which could be replaced by volume name or the path where we have given configuration to promeths
    ports:        # list of port mappings for services   (This makes it easy expose a service and access via http on any network interface)     .  'prometheus' can also replace with your preferred string. Here, Expose Ports are set accordingly in Dockerfile or you could use this block as part if the docker-compose command to specify ports explicitly
      - "9090:9090"   # Maps port 80 of service prometheus onto a container’s exposed port. This can be done because Promeths listens on these interfaces (default is localhost) . Change it according your needs, you could use 'localhost' for this or any specific IP as per requirement
    environment:          # Define all the required Environment Variables  - Grafana and Admin password would need to change appropriately if using in production   & will be exposed at runtime. Here is an example of what it might look like (replace with actual values)     . 'PROMETHEUS_CONFIG' can also replace as well
      - PROMETHEUS_CONFIG=/etc/prometheus  # where to store config file, could use relative path if docker-compose is run on the same directory   & Promeths would read it at startup. If this value changed in running environment then change these values accordingly    . 'GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD' needs replaced with actual password
        # image: prom/prometheus gives us a preconfigured Grafana instance  and we are exposing all the exposed ports within our container. This could be done in Dockerfile or part of docker-compose command file as per requirement, here it is shown for reference . Change them according to your needs
      - PROMETHEUS_CONFIG=/etc/prometheus  # where we store configuration at runtime   & Grafana would read this config when started. If the paths were changed in running environment then change these values accordingly    '. 'GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD' needs replaced with actual password
        . 'GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_USER=admin' could also be used if you want to set admin user name and Grafana Admin Password can replace it by your specific requirement. Here is an example of what this would look like (replace these values appropriately as per requirements)    
      - PROMETHEUS_CONFIG=${PROMETHEUS_CONFIG:-}/etc/prometheus   # if not provided, default to /var/lib/docker-volume which could be replaced by volume name or the path where we have given configuration. This is important for persisting data in case of container recreation
      - GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD=${GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-grafana}   # if not provided, default to 'monitor' (not recommended as passwords should be randomly generated and stored securely)  & will expose at runtime.
        . GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_USER=admin could also replace with actual username in case of specific requirements for security or you can use this block if need other admin user name while running Dockerfile/Compose file   here is an example, see comments above as it might be useful to understand the required format and structure.
       # image: grafana gives us a preconfigured Grafana instance  & we are exposing all exposed ports within our container too This can also happen in Dockerfile or part of docker-compose command file according requirement, here is shown for reference . Change them as per your needs    and the above points should be used to define services needed (Prometheus with Grafana)
